2605.07933.title: How to Train Your Latent Diffusion Language Model Jointly With the Latent Space
2605.07933.paper_summary: Latent diffusion models offer an attractive alternative to discrete diffusion for non-autoregressive text generation by operating on continuous text representations and denoising entire sequences in parallel. The major challenge in latent diffusion modeling is constructing a suitable latent space. In this work, we present the Latent Diffusion Language Model (LDLM), in which the latent encoder, diffusion model, and decoder are trained jointly. LDLM builds its latent space by reshaping the representations of a pre-trained language model with a trainable encoder, yielding latents that are easy to both denoise and decode into tokens. We show that naive joint training produces a low-quality diffusion model, and propose a simple training recipe consisting of an MSE decoder loss, diffusion-to-encoder warmup, adaptive timestep sampling, and decoder-input noise. Ablations show that each component substantially impacts generation performance. On OpenWebText and LM1B, LDLM achieves better generation performance than existing discrete and continuous diffusion language models while being $2{\text -}13\times$ faster, indicating that jointly learning the latent space is a key step toward making latent diffusion competitive for text generation.
2605.07933.paper_summary_zh: 潛在擴散模型為非自回歸文本生成提供了一個吸引人的替代方案，通過在連續文本表示上操作並平行去噪整個序列。潛在擴散建模的主要挑戰在於構建合適的潛在空間。在這項工作中，我們提出了潛在擴散語言模型（LDLM），其中潛在編碼器、擴散模型和解碼器是共同訓練的。LDLM通過使用可訓練的編碼器重塑預訓練語言模型的表示來構建其潛在空間，產生易於去噪和解碼為標記的潛在表示。我們展示了天真的聯合訓練會產生低質量的擴散模型，並提出了一個簡單的訓練方案，包括MSE解碼器損失、擴散到編碼器的預熱、自適應時間步採樣和解碼器輸入噪聲。消融實驗顯示每個組件對生成性能都有顯著影響。在OpenWebText和LM1B上，LDLM的生成性能優於現有的離散和連續擴散語言模型，同時速度快了$2{\text -}13\times$，這表明共同學習潛在空間是使潛在擴散在文本生成中具競爭力的關鍵步驟。
2605.07933.author: Viacheslav Meshchaninov et.al.
2605.07933.authors: Viacheslav Meshchaninov, Alexander Shabalin, Egor Chimbulatov, Nikita Gushchin, Ilya Koziev, Alexander Korotin, Dmitry Vetrov
